summary: Tested P2 features: PII Strict Mode (backend + frontend), App.jsx Decomposition verification, and existing pages regression. All 12 backend API tests passed. All frontend UI elements verified working correctly.
features_tested: POST /api/auth/login with demo@hotel.com / demo123 - Returns 200 with access_token
GET /api/security/pii-strict-mode/config - Returns config with enabled status
GET /api/security/pii-strict-mode/summary - Returns violation summary (total_violations, unique_paths, unique_users)
GET /api/security/pii-strict-mode/violations - Returns violations list with items array
GET /api/security/pii-strict-mode/encryption-status - Returns encryption coverage per collection
GET /api/security/pii-strict-mode/policy - Returns PII policy registry with categories
PII Strict Mode Dashboard UI (/pii-strict-mode) - Toggle switch, stat cards, encryption coverage, policy display, whitelist, recent events
App.jsx Decomposition - Dashboard loads correctly, routes work, sidebar navigation functional
Existing pages regression - /wire-failures, /exely, /hotelrunner all accessible and functional

issues: i18n translation keys showing as raw text (common.welcome, auth.signIn, etc.) - PRE-EXISTING issue from previous iterations, not caused by P2 changes

PII Strict Mode Dashboard shows encryption coverage per collection (guests at 99.6%)
App.jsx successfully decomposed from 2170 to 229 lines - routes in routeDefinitions.jsx, axios in axiosConfig.js, auth wrappers in ProtectedRoute.jsx
All navigation routes working correctly after decomposition
i18n translation keys issue on auth page is pre-existing, not caused by P2 changes

PII Strict Mode Dashboard at /pii-strict-mode shows toggle switch, violation stats, encryption coverage, PII policy, whitelist paths, and recent events.
